title: Known Screenshot Surrogate Conflicts for Generic Procurement Contract MVP
description: Evaluator-only UI/screenshot surrogate conflicts for testing optional screenshot semantic extraction and multi-source fusion. This file is not an AI input.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Contract detail UI uses Vendor for the external supplier party.
Purchase request detail UI uses Preferred Vendor.

conflicts.description: UI screens use Vendor labels while the database and canonical business term use Supplier.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Normalize Vendor to Supplier, preserve Vendor as UI alias, and do not create a separate Vendor concept.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit if the screenshot extraction records Vendor as an alias/mapping for Supplier. Penalize duplicate Vendor and Supplier concepts.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Invoice/payment UI uses GRN No for goods receipt reference.

conflicts.description: UI uses GRN while database and canonical ontology use Goods Receipt.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Normalize GRN to Goods Receipt and preserve it as an alias/abbreviation.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for mapping GRN to Goods Receipt. Penalize a separate GRN business concept unless marked as alias.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Approval task UI includes Return action.

conflicts.description: Return action appears in UI surrogate but is not part of the core MVP gold action set.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Treat Return as screenshot-only action candidate or uncertainty until confirmed by code/documents.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit if the output marks Return as screenshot-only or uncertain. Penalize if it is promoted to canonical backend action without provenance.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: UI shows Activate Contract for Procurement Admin after legal approval and date validation.
Contract activation policy must be reconciled with document and code rules.

conflicts.description: UI button visibility suggests who may activate contracts, but authorization must be confirmed by non-UI sources.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Record the role/action as UI evidence with low or candidate confidence unless supported by code or documents.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for distinguishing UI visibility from authoritative permission. Penalize declaring final permission solely from screenshot surrogate.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: UI displays Difference % but does not define the canonical tolerance threshold.
Policy defines tolerance terms outside the screenshot surrogate.
Code defines a specific tolerance constant outside the screenshot surrogate.

conflicts.description: The UI shows a calculated Difference % indicator but cannot by itself decide the canonical invoice matching tolerance threshold.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Treat Difference % as a UI signal for the matching process and defer threshold resolution to document/code fusion.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit if the output records the UI signal without inventing the threshold. Penalize if it claims a threshold from screenshot surrogate alone.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: UI shows Mark Paid and PAID status for payment requests.
DDL defines payment request statuses and should be used to confirm status vocabulary.

conflicts.description: UI-visible paid status and action need to be reconciled with the canonical payment status vocabulary.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Map UI PAID to canonical payment request paid state if supported by DDL/code; otherwise keep as UI-derived candidate.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for status mapping with evidence. Penalize merging invoice paid, payment request paid and purchase order closed states.
